title: Otis Worldwide (OTIS) Q4 Earnings Top, Sales Lag, Stock Down
text: Higher organic sales, operating profit growth and a lower effective tax rate help Otis Worldwide (OTIS) to post better-than-expected Q4 results.

title: Otis Worldwide tops fourth-quarter estimates
text: Otis Worldwide (NYSE: OTIS) stock shoots 4% after reporting $0.72 fourth-quarter EPS, which tops the $0.68 analyst estimate by $0.04. However, the company recorded revenue of $3.57 billion in the fourth quarter, less than the $3.5 billion consensus estimate.

title: Otis Worldwide Corporation (OTIS) CEO Judy Marks on Q4 2021 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Otis Worldwide Corporation (OTIS) CEO Judy Marks on Q4 2021 Results - Earnings Call Transcript

title: Otis Worldwide Stock Gains Post Q4 Results, Provides FY22 Guidance
text: Otis Worldwide Corp  OTIS reported fourth-quarter FY21 sales growth of 2.2% year-over-year to $3.569 billion, +2.8% on an organic basis, missing the consensus of $3.58 billion. New Equipment sales of $1.56 billion (+2% Y/Y), +1.2% organic.

title: Stocks Mixed to Start the Final Day of a Volatile Month
text: Stocks are mixed as a wild January wraps up; oil continues to climb, with $100-per-barrel predictions being issued.

title: Otis Worldwide Stock Rises After Mixed Results
text: Otis Worldwide Corp (NYSE:OTIS) is in the spotlight this morning, after the company's fourth-quarter report.

title: Otis stock gains after profit rises above expectations, while sales comes up a bit shy
text: Shares of Otis Worldwide Corp. OTIS, +0.19% gained 0.2% in premarket trading Monday, after the elevator and escalator maker reported fourth-quarter profit that topped expectations but sales that came up a bit shy, and provided a full-year sales outlook that was below forecasts. Net income rose to $281 million, or 65 cents a share, from $251 million, or 58 cents a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Dividend Growth Checkup - 2021
text: Analyzing my real-life portfolio's dividend growth for 2021. Dividend growth rebounded sharply in 2021, which was a more-than-welcomed sight. Weighted dividend growth came in at 8.7%, which is more than adequate.

title: Will Otis (OTIS) be Able to Deliver Higher Q4 Earnings?
text: Worldwide (OTIS) is likely to have witnessed higher revenues and earnings in the fourth quarter despite commodity inflation and the absence of temporary cost actions.

title: Librarian Capital's Monthly Top Buys And Price Moves - January 2022
text: Tobacco stocks remain strong buys after the defeat of the U.S. "nicotine tax" proposal and a reassuring update from British American Tobacco. In Payment, Mastercard and Visa each rebounded by more than 10%; U.S. holiday retail sales were strong and credit cards kept growing.
